---
title: "What If: Causal Inference"
subtitle: "Lecture Notes and Slides"
---

## Welcome

Welcome to the lecture notes and slides based on **"What If"** by Miguel Hernán and James Robins.

This website provides:

- **HTML Lecture Notes**: Detailed written materials with examples and explanations
- **RevealJS Slides**: Presentation slides for each lecture topic

## About the Book

"What If" (also known as "Causal Inference: What If") is a comprehensive textbook on causal inference methods. The book is freely available at [https://www.hsph.harvard.edu/miguel-hernan/causal-inference-book/](https://www.hsph.harvard.edu/miguel-hernan/causal-inference-book/).

## Course Topics

This course covers fundamental concepts in causal inference including:

1. **Introduction to Causal Inference**
   - Causal effects and counterfactuals
   - Randomization and exchangeability
   
2. **Randomized Experiments**
   - Design principles
   - Analysis methods
   - Limitations
   
3. **Observational Studies**
   - Confounding
   - Selection bias
   - Adjustment strategies

## Navigation

Use the navigation bar above to access:

- **Lectures**: Detailed HTML notes for each topic
- **Slides**: RevealJS presentation slides

## Resources

- [Book Website](https://miguelhernan.org/whatifbook)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/ucdavis/win)
